channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: URGENT: Safety Hazard / Breach of PSPO – Aggressive Unleashed Dogs, 27th May
item.description:
I am urgently reporting a severe and recurring public safety hazard at Penge Recreation Ground involving a repeat offender with two highly aggressive, completely unleashed dogs.This individual and his dogs are in direct violation of the London Borough of Bromley Public Spaces Protection Order (PSPO) 2023, which mandates that dogs must be kept under proper control and prohibits anti-social behaviour.Incident Details: This specific dog owner handles two large, highly aggressive Staffordshire Bull Terriers (one tan-coloured, one black-coloured).Both large dogs are routinely permitted to roam completely off-lead. The owner intentionally utilizes these unleashed dogs as a mechanism to intimidate, threaten, and harass park users. Last night, these dogs were used to escalate a targeted, aggressive confrontation against me. This individual is a known regular at Penge Recreation Ground in relation with other incidents. Given the high volume of children, families, and other dog walkers using this park, these aggressive, unrestrained animals pose an immediate physical threat to the public.I request that your park wardens and enforcement teams immediately increase targeted patrols at Penge Recreation Ground—particularly during evening hours—to identify this individual, enforce the PSPO rules, and coordinate with the Penge & Cator Safer Neighbourhoods policing team.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Avington Grove, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped rubbish from large delivery / pallets, 27th May
item.description:
Broken pallet / nails and wrapping left in street outside our building.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Laxley Close, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Norwood Road SE24 / Dulwich Road junction –, 27th May
item.description:
Norwood Road SE24 / Dulwich Road junction – Brockwell Park Herne Hill gate entrance. Either side of this park gate as of this morning were 15 to 20 – flyposters attached to the park railings - A3 polycarbonate boards secured with plastic zip ties so will need a knife and ‘snipes’ to remove. Google Streetview of gate entrance: https://www.google.com/maps/@51.4519532,-0.1015955,3a,60y,274.87h,88.71t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sSD3OyAvLt7wKhf0yvtd9GA!2e0!6shttps:%2F%2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com%2Fv1%2Fthumbnail%3Fcb_client%3Dmaps_sv.tactile%26w%3D900%26h%3D600%26pitch%3D1.2862876869107538%26panoid%3DSD3OyAvLt7wKhf0yvtd9GA%26yaw%3D274.8699237072506!7i13312!8i6656?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI2MDUyMC4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dulwich Road, Herne Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking parking spaces, 27th May
item.description:
On 27/05/26 at 10h40, The owner at 152 Bethune Road blocked the parking spaces with bins and doors making it unsafe and as far as I know illegal. It appears that the space has been blocked for contractors.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bethune Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

Vehicle abandoned on the pavement over 2 weeks .

item.title: Settee Dumped outside a bin store of a block of flats, 27th May
item.description:
A Settee has been dumped today between 11.30 and 12 outside of a block of flats, blocking parking spaces and the bin store and is now being used by kids

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 33, Old Farm Place, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven pavement slab creating a raised edge and trip hazard, 27th May
item.description:
Dangerous uneven pavement slab located directly outside Bedales of Borough. The slab has become raised and unstable around a utility cover, creating a significant trip hazard for pedestrians. This issue has already caused multiple customers and passers-by to trip while leaving the premises or walking along the pavement. The raised edge and gap are particularly difficult to notice and present a serious safety risk, especially during busy periods.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2-4, Bedale Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Dumped within the last few days

item.title: Very bad road surface, 27th May
item.description:
The road from the zebra crossing down towards streatham Hill Station has become very pothole filled and uneven. Cars are dodging the potholes and it's dangerous for cyclists.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leigham Court Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London

item.title: Dead pigeon, 27th May
item.description:
Please remove

item.title: Flytipping, 27th May
item.description:
Someone has emptied a flat or garage and the area around the communal bins on the corner of Marlborough Avenue/Brownlow Road are full of furniture and other waste making it difficult to get to the bins. The recycling bins are also overflowing
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marlborough Avenue, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 big potholes, 27th May
item.description:
3 very large potholes in a triangular placing. One very deep, making it quite dangerous for cyclists and cars can get damaged. They need to be filled in as a matter of urgency.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Broxholm Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: N36/38 Wellington Gardens continuing flytipping, 27th May
item.description:
Witnessed a person chunking their stuff on the sideway, old dishes,boxes, cardboards, toys. So now other people in the area are doing the same. This is happening at n 36.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wellington Gardens, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped vehicle with no MoT or Road Tax, 27th May
item.description:
Vehicle was dumped a few weeks ago and has not moved

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Rise, East Barnet, Greater London

item.title: Home improvements done somewhere and dumped furniture and wood, 27th May
item.description:
Been there for couple months now and getting worse every week start of the alley behind the coop by metal gates

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Weyman Road, Kidbrooke

item.title: Overhanging on private property.blocking sunlight, 27th May
item.description:
About 3 years ago I was told trees will be trimmed as they were developing the entrance to pier.been in touch before nothing has yet been done.theres also all the trees surrounding morissons and opposite matalan are quite dangerous walking past them. Regards len butler

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Waters Edge Court, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
